name: DemoFoundry — Feature tour
description: The full flagship tour: the New-demo flow, the Voice screen (cloning your own voice, the read-aloud popup, the pacing sliders), and the polished finished output. One target per scene (highlight only, no tight zooms). The Voice screen needs a project (__PROJECT_ID__) and the finish needs a rendered project (__DONE_PROJECT_ID__) — see the sample-scripts README.

steps.narration_text: This is DemoFoundry — and this demo was made by DemoFoundry, driving its own interface. We start in the Library, where every project lives.
To begin a new demo, you click New demo.
First you pick how to capture. A web app gets driven automatically in a browser. A desktop app you record yourself. We're demoing a web app.
You name the demo — the label you'll see back in your Library.
Then you point it at the app you want to demo — usually your own app on localhost.
You drop in a script — an ordered list of actions and the words to narrate — and DemoFoundry reads it back live, showing every scene it understood.
Once you've captured your app, you choose how it sounds. This is the Voice screen — a whole catalog of narrators, each with a preview you can play.
But the best voice might be your own. Click Use my voice, and you can record a one-minute sample — or upload one — and DemoFoundry clones it, so every demo sounds like you.
Not sure what to say? There's a script right here — about a minute of natural speech to read aloud while you record, so the clone comes out clean.
And two sliders set the pace: how quickly the narration speaks, and how long each new screen holds before the voice begins — so nothing ever feels rushed.
Then you hit render, and this is the finished result: a polished, captioned MP4, ready to download or play right here — narrated in the voice you chose, paced exactly how you set it.
That's the whole loop. Point DemoFoundry at your app, give it a script, choose a voice, and ship a demo that looks like you spent days on it — in minutes.
